components.templateData:
Home / Components / Button

Angular Button Component

Build accessible, themeable call-to-action buttons for Angular 21 applications. Premium UI buttons support multiple variants, loading states, icon slots, and full keyboard interaction — ideal for SaaS dashboards, forms, and marketing surfaces.

@for (tab of tabs; track tab.label) { {{ tab.label }} }
@switch (currentTab()) { @case ('overview') {

# Overview

Buttons are the primary action control in Angular interfaces. Use them to submit forms, confirm dialogs, navigate workflows, and trigger commands. Premium UI ships pui-button as a standalone Angular component with OnPush change detection, signal-friendly inputs, and semantic tokens for light and dark themes.

Choose a variant to communicate hierarchy: primary for the main action, secondary or outline for supporting actions, and ghost for low-emphasis toolbar controls. Pair buttons with inputs and checkboxes in form layouts.

components.templateData:
Home / Components / Select

Select

A feature-driven combobox for single and multi selection with search, virtualization, async loading, and full keyboard support.

@for (tab of tabs; track tab.label) { {{ tab.label }} }
@switch (currentTab()) { @case ('overview') {

Overview

Enable features declaratively through inputs. Combine searchable, multiple, and virtualScroll as needed without separate component variants.

Selected: {{ selectedFramework() }}

@for (size of sizes; track size) { }

Features

  • Single and multi select with clearable values
  • Local and async search with debounced searchChange events
  • Virtual scroll for 10k+ options via Angular CDK
  • Custom option and empty templates
  • Disabled options, loading, and empty states
  • ARIA combobox with active descendant keyboard navigation
} @case ('examples') {

Examples

Common patterns

@for (example of examples; track example.title; let i = $index) { }

Parent integration

Selection state is owned by the parent via [(value)], signals, or Angular forms. The trigger label is always derived from the current value — never duplicated internally.

Angular React Vue Angular React Vue
} @case ('api') {

API Guide

} @case ('accessibility') {

Accessibility

Keyboard and ARIA

} @case ('theming') {

Theming

CSS variables

Customize appearance without modifying component source using semantic tokens.

} @case ('performance') {

Performance

Scalable rendering and worker processing

Premium UI separates computation from rendering. Enable virtualScroll for DOM efficiency and opt into useWorker to delegate heavy search/filter work off the main thread.

What workers handle

  • Text search, fuzzy search, keyword search
  • Filtering, sorting, grouping, ranking
  • Large dataset indexing and transformations

What workers do NOT handle

  • DOM rendering or virtual scroll (stays on main thread)
  • Overlays, focus management, keyboard navigation, animations

Datasets are initialized once inside the worker. Subsequent operations send only query payloads — not the full options array — to minimize serialization cost.

Implementation notes

  • Features initialize lazily — search UI renders only when searchable and open
  • OnPush change detection with signal-derived filtered options
  • Debounced worker search with cancellation for stale queries
  • Automatic main-thread fallback when workers are unavailable
  • Fixed itemHeight for predictable virtual scroll measurements
} @case ('playground') {

components.templateData:
Home / Components / Toast

Angular Toast System

Non-blocking feedback for save confirmations, async uploads, undo actions, and system alerts. Toasts are orchestrated through PuiToastService, rendered in stacked viewports, and styled with semantic Lucide icons and subtle tinting — built for production SaaS apps.

@for (tab of tabs; track tab.label) { {{ tab.label }} }
@switch (currentTab()) { @case ('overview') {

Overview

Inject PuiToastService anywhere in your app and call a semantic method. Mount pui-toast-viewport once at the root — the service handles stacking, timers, and accessibility for you.

Use string messages for quick feedback, or pass a PuiToastInput object when you need titles, descriptions, custom icons, or action buttons in one call.

Show success toast Show object toast

Installation

Add the viewport beside your router outlet. The service is provided in root — no module imports required beyond the viewport component itself.

When to use toasts

  • Confirmations — save, copy, delete with optional undo
  • Async workflows — loading → success/error via stable ids or promise()
  • Low-attention feedback — compact and snackbar modes for transient messages
  • Rich announcements — title + description + CTA for deployments and billing events
} @case ('examples') {

Variants & icons

Each semantic variant maps to a Lucide-aligned outline icon: success → circle-check, error → circle-alert, warning → triangle-alert, info → info, loading → loader-circle. Override with iconName or hide icons with icon: false.

Success Error Warning Info
Default icon Custom icon Iconless
Show snackbar Show rich toast Run loading demo Run upload demo

Positions

Each toast targets one of six viewport anchors. Snackbars default to bottom-center; all other variants default to bottom-right unless you pass position.

@for (position of positions; track position) { {{ position }} }
} @case ('api') {

Service API

All methods accept PuiToastMessage — either a string or a PuiToastInput object. Options merge with variant defaults.

} @case ('accessibility') {

Accessibility

Toasts use live regions so screen readers announce feedback without stealing focus. Action buttons remain keyboard reachable; dismiss controls expose an accessible label.

} @case ('theming') {

Theming

Toast surfaces use semantic CSS variables for accent, background tint, and border color. Override tokens globally or per theme — avoid hard-coded colors in component SCSS.

} @case ('playground') {

Playground

Adjust variant, position, duration, icons, and actions — then copy the generated HTML and TypeScript snippets into your project.

Variant
Position
Duration (ms)
Snackbar mode Rich mode Action button Show icon Custom bell icon Loading transition

Trigger

Show toast
} }

components.templateData:
Home / Components / Toggle

Toggle

Premium pressed-state controls — segmented selectors, editor toolbars, and filter chips with sliding indicators and spring motion.

@for (tab of tabs; track tab.label) { {{ tab.label }} }
@switch (currentTab()) { @case ('overview') {

Overview

Toggles represent pressed/unpressed action states — formatting controls, view switchers, segmented selectors, and filter chips. They use aria-pressed and feel like a unified surface, not separate buttons.

Toggle vs Switch

  • Switch — binary on/off settings (notifications, dark mode). Uses role="switch".
  • Toggle — pressed action state (Bold, Grid view). Uses role="button" + aria-pressed.

Segmented vs Toolbar

  • Segmented (mode="segmented") — single selection with sliding active pill. Grid/List, Week/Month/Year.
  • Toolbar (mode="toolbar") — multi-select compact icon controls. Bold/Italic/Underline.
Grid List Compact B I U
} @case ('variants') {
